candidate_summary: BASHO-ROTATION weighted fixed-schedule coordinator rotation through K=4 candidate agents with central agent over-weighted at 50% of rounds (Tokyo-Osaka-Tokyo-Nagoya-Tokyo-Fukuoka pattern). Recombines (a) fixed pre-scheduled rotation + (b) weighted central leader + (c) K=4 candidate pool.

results_analysis.title: Reaching Agreement Among Reasoning LLM Agents (2025/12)
results_analysis.note: Explicitly names round-robin queues and elected leaders as predictable coordinator schemes — directly overlaps fixed-schedule rotation + leader election components.

results_analysis.title: Coordination Mechanisms in MAS
results_analysis.note: Dedicated Coordinator Agent with pre-determined leader covers BASHO's central-coordinator-with-rotation pattern.

results_analysis.title: AgentsNet: Coordination + Collaborative Reasoning (2025/07)
results_analysis.note: Leader election + consensus as fundamental MAS primitives subsumes BASHO's leader selection.

results_analysis.title: Byzantine-Robust Decentralized Coordination of LLM Agents (2025/07)
results_analysis.note: Discusses leader-driven coordination space directly relevant to BASHO's weighted leader rotation.

results_analysis.title: 6 Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns for Production (2026)
results_analysis.note: Hierarchical Supervisor-Worker pattern matches BASHO's central-leader-with-peripheral-workers architecture.

results_analysis.title: Voting or Consensus? Multi-Agent Decision-Making (ACL 2025)
results_analysis.note: Kw>=2 and sem>=0.7 — passes despite weaker functional match (decision protocols vs leader selection).

results_analysis.title: Agent Orchestration Patterns: Swarm vs Mesh vs Hierarchical
results_analysis.note: Generic orchestration survey below all thresholds.

results_analysis.title: RL for LLM-based MAS through Orchestration Traces (2026/05)
results_analysis.note: Kw>=2 on orchestrator/orchestration — passes despite sem<0.7 and func<0.7.

verdict_sentence: BASHO-ROTATION fails novelty: 7 of 8 retrieved sources hit under the (kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7) rule, far exceeding the failure threshold of 1; prior art explicitly covers round-robin coordinator queues, elected leaders, pre-determined leader schemes, and hierarchical supervisor-worker orchestration — the three components (fixed schedule + weighted central leader + small candidate pool) are all individually documented in the MAS coordination literature.
